Groundwater Depletion Assessment and Sus: This domain examines the causes, measurement, and management of groundwater depletion across India, drawing on evidence from Granger causality analyses, GRACE-style storage estimates in the Ganga Basin, and studies linking warming temperatures and cropping intensity to aquifer decline. It also covers assessment tools like satellite-based irrigation detection, hydro-economic optimization of crop patterns, and characterization of the subcontinent's groundwater systems and deeper aquifers. Finally, it addresses sustainable governance and community-based solutions, including system dynamics models for village-level water use and farm-pond intensification.
Rainwater Harvesting and Traditional Tan: This domain focuses on capturing and recharging water through both engineered and traditional structures, including watershed-based tank systems, percolation tanks, and ancient tank cascade systems in semi-arid Southern India. The readings assess the effectiveness and sustainability of these recharge interventions and identify suitable zones for rainwater harvesting to support agricultural intensification in the semi-arid tropics.
Satellite Remote Sensing and ML for Wate: This domain covers machine learning methods applied to satellite imagery for mapping water, land, and infrastructure features, spanning object detection (ship detection in SAR, wastewater-treatment monitoring), segmentation, and irrigation-type mapping. It includes tooling and techniques such as OpenMapFlow, data augmentation, attention-based scattering networks, and knowledge-informed deep learning, alongside emerging capabilities like geospatial chain-of-thought reasoning and frame prediction for geostationary imagery.
Precipitation, Weather, and Climate Fore: This domain encompasses deep learning approaches for weather and climate prediction, including precipitation nowcasting, subseasonal and long-range forecasting, temperature prediction, and extreme weather detection using RNNs, graph neural networks, diffusion models, and autoregressive architectures. It also covers benchmark datasets (WeatherBench, RainBench, ClimateNet), climate data generation, uncertainty handling, and self-supervised representation learning for high-dimensional weather and climate data.
Flood and Drought Risk Modeling and Impa: This domain addresses the modeling of flood and drought risks and their societal impacts, combining hydrological AI models, graph neural network flood prediction, and multimodal machine learning approaches. It emphasizes human and infrastructure dimensions, including damage assessment, mobility patterns, socially informed drought estimation, and flood exposure in vulnerable regions like Bangladesh, as well as decision-support and monitoring systems for flood response.
Climate Resilience Tools and Participato: This domain focuses on participatory tools and frameworks for assessing climate vulnerability and building resilience in rural communities, including CoDriVE, Commons Connect, and the CRISP-M tool for MGNREGA planning. It covers community consultation on natural resource management, retrospective resilience assessment of semi-arid farming systems, and field testing of digital tools for water security, linking pro-poor social protection policies to climate adaptation.
Nature-Based Solutions and Landscape/Wat: This domain explores ecological and systems-based approaches to landscape and watershed planning, including watershed integrity assessment, landscape ecology metrics, and system dynamics modeling of irrigation efficiency and hydrologic-economic effects. It also examines mental models for water sharing in micro-irrigation, ecological economics modeling, and the broader value and limits of nature-based solutions for addressing climate change and degraded Indian landscapes.
AI Flood Mapping and Forecasting from Sa: This domain applies AI to detect and map floods from satellite imagery, leveraging Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical data, foundation models, semi-supervised learning, and diffusion-based SAR-to-EO translation. It includes large-scale surface water datasets, flood prediction in sparse-observation settings like Kenya, crowdsourcing-enhanced maps, and analyses of human impacts such as mobility and destruction of populated sites during flooding in Nigeria.
Streamflow and Hydrological Modeling wit: This domain covers machine learning methods for predicting streamflow and runoff, including LSTMs, transformers, graph neural networks, and vision transformers, often applied in data-sparse or data-scarce regions such as Benin and the Ansongo-Niamey Basin. It emphasizes techniques like domain adaptation, fine-tuning global models with local data, missing-data imputation, and theory-guided or physics-informed learning for flood, drought, and flash flood forecasting.
Remote Sensing Foundation Models and Seg: This domain focuses on foundation models and advanced segmentation techniques for remote sensing, including lightweight pretrained transformers for timeseries, visual prompt-tuning, and adaptations of the Segment Anything Model for change detection. It also covers few-shot instance segmentation, unsupervised change detection for disaster management, geographic location encoders, and machine learning toolkits for Earth system data cubes and coastline delineation.
Tropical Cyclone and Extreme Storm Predi: This domain focuses on applying artificial intelligence and generative models to forecast tropical cyclones and their associated hazards. Readings cover techniques such as conditional denoising diffusion models for estimating atmospheric variables from typhoon satellite images, Pix2Pix GANs for wind field diagnosis, and video diffusion models for improved cyclone forecasting. It also addresses 3D cloud reconstruction and enhancing the spatial accuracy of extreme cyclone rainfall in reanalysis data using deep learning.
Coastal Change and Shoreline Forecasting: This domain examines deep learning approaches for predicting coastal flooding and long-term shoreline evolution under climate change. Readings include climate adaptation-aware and vision-based frameworks for coastal flood prediction that account for shoreline adaptations, as well as forecasting global coastline evolution directly from satellite imagery.
Climate Downscaling and Simulation Corre: This domain addresses machine learning methods for correcting biases, downscaling, and emulating climate and Earth system simulations. Readings cover adaptive bias correction for subseasonal forecasting, contrastive learning and image-to-image translation for debiasing and super-resolution, and diffusion-based emulation and downscaling for extreme-event simulation. Additional topics include neural representations for ocean forecasting, radiative transfer modeling, ice flow prediction, and reconstructing land surface boundary conditions.
Compound Climate Extremes and Disaster H: This domain covers the detection and prediction of concurrent and interacting climate hazards such as wildfires, droughts, and heatwaves using spatiotemporal deep learning and remote sensing. Readings include burned area mapping and short-term fire hazard prediction, identifying atmospheric drivers of drought and heat, and predicting the concurrence of heatwaves, droughts, and wildfires. It also spans agent-based climate risk modeling, vision transformers for early climate signal detection, and lightweight networks for extreme weather detection.
AI for Climate Policy and Ecosystem Valu: This domain explores AI applications for informing climate policy, valuing ecosystems, and supporting environmental and climate justice. Readings include causal frameworks for ecosystem valuation such as wetland flood mitigation benefits, emulating integrated assessment models, and making climate AI systems past- and future-aware for policy evaluation. It further covers carbon footprint perception, real-time emission management in Kenya, environmental justice policy in Atlanta, climate knowledge bases, and explainable geospatial predictions for climate finance.
Drought Forecasting and Impact Monitorin: This domain focuses on forecasting drought severity and duration and monitoring its impacts using deep learning across diverse climate zones. Readings include dedicated datasets like DroughtSet and DroughtED, hybrid recurrent networks, specialized loss functions such as SQUASH, and interpretable methods for extreme-event detection. It also emphasizes localized and socially grounded applications, including Indigenous knowledge-aware monitoring, support for pastoral communities in Northern Kenya, and Twitter-based impact recognition.
Weather and Climate Model Emulation and: This domain covers machine learning models that emulate, calibrate, and downscale weather and climate simulations. Readings include benchmark datasets for meteorological downscaling, foundation models such as ClimaX, and emulation of local-scale UK climate models. It also addresses uncertainty quantification and calibration in long-range forecasting with large neural weather models.
Extreme Weather Event Detection and Fore: This domain addresses identifying and forecasting extreme weather and hydrological events under a changing climate. Readings include Bayesian inference of severe hail in Australia, lightweight neural networks for improved extreme weather detection, and machine learning approaches for identifying hydrological extremes under global climate change.
Satellite Land Cover and Water Body Segm: This domain focuses on segmentation and classification of land cover, land use, and water bodies from satellite and aerial imagery using deep learning. Readings cover large-scale surface water detection, wetland delineation with imbalanced segmentation, aquaculture pond mapping, irrigation detection and method mapping, and solar plant mapping. It also spans methodological advances including data augmentation, few-shot transfer learning, self-supervised and semi-supervised methods, contextual and geographical priors, and scalable cloud-based LULC classification.
Social Media and Crowdsourced Disaster R: This domain examines the use of social media, crowdsourced, and citizen-reported data for understanding and responding to climate disasters. Readings include analyzing Twitter discourse around Cyclone Amphan, improving flood maps through crowdsourcing and semantic segmentation, and recognizing drought impacts from Twitter data and drought impact reports using deep learning and XGBoost.
Flood & Disaster Risk Mapping and Respon: This domain examines technological systems for assessing, monitoring, and responding to disasters, including satellite-based damage assessment and risk monitoring. It covers specialized infrastructure such as nationwide lightning detection networks for weather forecasting in the Philippines, as well as broader systems architecture approaches to disaster risk reduction.
Rural Water Supply Infrastructure Monito: This domain focuses on tracking the functionality and usage of rural water infrastructure, exemplified by electronic sensors deployed in Plateau State, Nigeria. It also addresses digital mapping platforms, such as community water system mapping, designed to support the operators who manage rural water systems.
Drinking Water Quality, Safety & Contami: This domain addresses drinking water safety through interventions targeting contamination, including arsenic-safe water technology and willingness-to-pay studies in West Bengal, India. It also covers technological solutions like air-to-water generators in Kerala and investigations into urban underground water contamination.
Community-Based & Indigenous Knowledge C: This domain explores how communities and indigenous knowledge contribute to climate resilience, including profiling disaster-stricken communities and digitizing indigenous knowledge. It emphasizes participatory and co-design approaches, such as empowering Bangladeshi communities on water safety and co-designing microclimate sensors.
Rainwater Harvesting & Off-Grid Water/Ag: This domain covers water conservation and rainwater collection technologies for sustainability, with case studies in Guatemala and Northern India. It examines the agricultural impact of farm ponds and the integration of rainwater harvesting with automated off-grid irrigation for food and water security.
Gender, Empowerment & Marginalization in: This domain examines the intersection of gender empowerment and water-related programs, including vetiver grass technology for river restoration and women's empowerment in rural India. It highlights training rural women to monitor water quality and integrating agriculture with clean energy to enhance livelihoods and WASH empowerment.
Satellite-Derived Socio-Economic & Vulne: This domain uses satellite imagery and remote sensing to map socio-economic indicators, poverty, and vulnerability at high resolution. It covers methods including point-to-region co-learning, temporal prediction, time series analysis, and nighttime light reconstruction, with applications spanning Africa, rural India, and urban disadvantaged populations in the United States.
Satellite Imagery Analysis for Environme: This domain applies deep learning and machine learning to satellite imagery for environmental monitoring, including landslide detection, glacier monitoring, soil carbon estimation, and vegetation greenness forecasting. It also addresses methodological challenges such as domain gaps, interpretability, and data augmentation, with studies spanning Lima, the Hindu Kush Himalaya, India, and Karachi.
Computational Hydrology and Terrain Anal: This domain focuses on computational tools and algorithms for analyzing terrain and hydrological features, including deep learning for geomorphological mapping and gully erosion susceptibility. It covers topographic position and landform analysis as well as GPU-accelerated hydrology algorithms for flow accumulation, watershed delineation, and runoff simulation.
